text.title: OpenAI API Integration — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `openai-api-integration` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Model selection MUST match task complexity. Start at gpt-4o-mini; escalate only if quality tests fail. Never default to gpt-4-turbo.
rule.rationale: Cost and capability differ by ~30x across the lineup; starting cheap and escalating is the only way to keep $/task predictable.

rule.statement: Wrap responses in Pydantic models (or zod). Raw `response.choices[0].message.content.strip()` access is rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Untyped string handling drops field defaults, silently swallows tool-call payloads, and makes structured-output migrations 10× harder.

rule.statement: Wrap every API call in retry-with-exponential-backoff on RateLimitError, APIConnectionError, and 5xx APIError; cap at 4 attempts.
rule.rationale: Transient failures are the dominant production error class; without bounded retries an agent either hangs forever or fails on the first hiccup.

rule.statement: When sending ≥5 prompts concurrently, use AsyncOpenAI + asyncio.gather; never busy-loop sync calls.
rule.rationale: AsyncOpenAI multiplexes over a shared connection pool; sync loops blow connection limits and serialise latency.

rule.statement: Every production call records (model, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, $cost) to telemetry. No `print()` budget tracking.
rule.rationale: Without structured cost telemetry, a Friday-night infinite loop costs four figures before anyone notices on Monday.
